evals.description: Trigger test: activates on k6 load test question
evals.prompt: I want to load test my REST API with k6. Where do I start?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on stress testing question
evals.prompt: How do I find the breaking point of my API under stress?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on soak testing question
evals.prompt: We suspect our app has a memory leak but it only shows up after hours of traffic. How should I test this?

evals.description: Core task: implements ramping stages in k6
evals.prompt: Write a k6 script that ramps from 0 to 100 virtual users over 2 minutes, holds for 5 minutes, then ramps back down.

evals.description: Core task: defines pass/fail thresholds before running
evals.prompt: How do I make my k6 test automatically fail if p95 latency exceeds 500ms or the error rate goes above 1%?

evals.description: Core task: uses SharedArray for parameterized test data
evals.prompt: My k6 test always hits the same user ID, so it's just testing the cache. How do I load a list of real user IDs and randomize which one each VU uses?

evals.description: Core task: integrates k6 into GitHub Actions CI
evals.prompt: How do I run k6 load tests automatically in GitHub Actions on every push to main?

evals.description: Core task: reads and interprets k6 output correctly
evals.prompt: My k6 run shows p95=200ms but p99=1800ms. What does this gap mean and how do I find the root cause?

evals.description: Core task: models a realistic multi-step user journey
evals.prompt: I need to load test a checkout flow: login, browse products, add to cart, place order. How do I model this as a single k6 scenario?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against using averages for performance decisions
evals.prompt: Our load test shows average response time is 120ms which seems good. Can we say the service passes performance requirements?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against testing without think time
evals.prompt: I set my k6 test to 100 VUs with no sleep between requests to maximize RPS. Is this a good approach?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend testing in production without safeguards
evals.prompt: Should I just run my load test against production to get the most realistic results?

assertions.value: production is always the best choice
